channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bournvita supports Nigerian Muslim families with nourishment and community outreach this Ramadan
item.description: For the holy month of Ramadan, Bournvita, Nigeria’s popular cocoa drink brand, don bring out one large community outreach program wey go touch more than 150,000 Nigerians. The 2026 campaign, wey dem c...

item.title: Akwa Ibom Government Clears N39.83 Billion Debt and Reduces Gratuity Backlog to N80 Billion
item.description: The Akwa Ibom State Government don announce say dem don clear all inherited bank debts wey reach N39.83 billion, including principal and interest. Na Mr. Emem Bob, the Commissioner for Finance, talk t...

item.title: Trump’s Wealth Rises to $6.5 Billion, Driven by Crypto and Real Estate Gains
item.description: According to a recent Forbes report cited by The Independent, President Donald Trump’s personal fortune has surged to approximately $6.5 billion, marking an increase of around $1.4 billion over the pa...

item.title: Google, Tesla, and Partners Launch Utilize Coalition to Advocate for Better Utilization of Existing Electrical Grid Capacity
item.description: On March 10, 2026, a new industry coalition named Utilize was launched by companies including Google, Tesla, data center developer Verrus, HVAC manufacturer Carrier, virtual power plant firm Renew Hom...

item.title: Gauteng provincial budget 2026/2027 allocates funds to key departments and political parties
item.description: Gauteng Finance MEC, Lebogang Maile, tabled the province’s 2026/2027 budget at R179 billion, marking a R3.6 billion increase from last year. Of this, R1.4 billion has been allocated to the provincial ...

item.title: Developments in Israel-Iran conflict: U.S. naval strikes, UAE alerts, and regional tensions
item.description: The ongoing conflict between Israel and Iran continues to escalate, with multiple international interventions and regional concerns. On March 10, 2026, U.S. President Donald Trump announced that the U...

item.title: Sandiganbayan denies bail for ex-DPWH officials in P289.5M Mindoro flood project case
item.description: The Sandiganbayan has rejected the bail petitions of nine former officials from the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) in the Mimaropa region over their involvement in a P289.5-million sub...

item.title: Delta State Launches Three-Month World Book Day Festival to Promote Literature and Libraries
item.description: Delta State don launch the LightRay! Distortion World Book Day Festival under the theme “That Which Bends, Transcends.” The event na celebration of literature, storytelling, and culture for both rich ...

item.title: Trump and Netanyahu’s military strikes on Iran: Risks and uncertainties
item.description: Thomas L. Friedman, writing for The New York Times, argues that the Trump-Netanyahu military strategy against Iran carries serious risks and uncertain outcomes. While their air strikes have temporaril...

item.title: Government Launches Multi-Billion Irrigation Projects to Support Rukwa Farmers
item.description: The Tanzanian government has promised to support farmers in Rukwa Region through irrigation projects valued at 51.9 billion shillings. During a visit by Prime Minister Mwigulu Nchemba in Nkasi Distric...

item.title: Mo Dewji retains position as East and Central Africa’s wealthiest businessman despite Forbes ranking drop
item.description: Tanzanian tycoon Mohammed Dewji has held onto his title as the richest individual in East and Central Africa, even after a slight decline in his fortune according to Forbes' 2026 ranking of African bi...

item.title: Ruto Defends UDA-ODM Cooperation, Calls for Direct Accountability to Kenyans
item.description: During a joint parliamentary group meeting at the Kenyatta International Convention Centre, President William Ruto reprimanded what he called 'self-appointed supervisors' of the cooperation between UD...

item.title: Phone Records Suggest Communication Between Kidnapper and Ebonyi Project Officials Before Engineers Went Missing
item.description: Tracking records don show say notorious kidnapper wey dem dey call 'Small', wey dey linked to the disappearance or even possible killing of five engineers wey dey work for Abakaliki Ring Road project ...

item.title: Environment Canada Issues Warning for Significant Freezing Rain in Southern Quebec
item.description: Environment Canada has issued an orange alert for a major freezing rain storm expected to affect southern Quebec, parts of Ontario, and the Atlantic provinces. The storm, predicted to begin around 6 a...

item.title: Mbeere North Election Petitioner Endures Intense Cross-Examination in Court
item.description: In Embu High Court, the election petition challenging the Mbeere North by-election results saw tense moments as petitioner Julieta Karigi Kithumbu faced sharp cross-examination from lawyers representi...

item.title: High-Risk Severe Thunderstorm and Tornado Outbreak Forecast for Central US on March 10, 2026
item.description: Forecasters have issued a Level 4 of 5 severe thunderstorm risk—the highest in months—for parts of northern Illinois and northwest Indiana, affecting over 2 million people in areas including Peoria, I...

item.title: Seventh Suspect Surrenders in Cavite Student Hazing Case
item.description: Following last week's surrender of six individuals, another member of the Tau Gamma Phi fraternity, Dar Sabilona, has turned himself in to the Criminal Investigation and Detection Group-National Capit...

item.title: Qantas and Other Airlines Raise International Fares Amid Soaring Jet Fuel Prices
item.description: Qantas International has announced fare increases on its international routes in response to a sharp rise in jet fuel costs, which have surged by 150 per cent since the conflict in the Middle East beg...

item.title: Former IRA explosives expert claims Gerry Adams wore black beret at IRA funeral in High Court trial
item.description: A photograph showing Gerry Adams wearing a black beret while marching in an 'honour guard' at the funeral of IRA member Michael Kane in 1971 has been presented as evidence in a London High Court trial...

item.title: Returning to Beirut Amid Escalating Conflict
item.description: Journalist Eric Reguly recounts his recent journey back to Beirut amid renewed conflict between Israel and Hezbollah. Previously, in October 2024, Reguly witnessed the devastation in southern Lebanon ...
